name: Cranberry Eggnog Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
For the Crust:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Combine cookie crumbs and butter. Press onto the bottom and up the sides of a 9-inch springform pan.
3. Bake for 8-10 minutes or until golden. Cool completely.
For the Filling:
1. In a medium-sized heavy saucepan, combine 1/2 cup sugar, gelatin, and salt. Beat egg yolks slightly and combine with eggnog; gradually stir into the gelatin mixture.
2. Cook over low to medium heat, stirring constantly, until gelatin is dissolved and thickened. Do not boil. Remove from heat and cool slightly.
3. Beat cream cheese, orange peel, and vanilla until fluffy. Stir in the cooked mixture until well blended.
4. Refrigerate until the mixture mounds when dropped from a spoon.
5. In a large bowl, beat egg whites and cream of tartar until frothy. Gradually beat in remaining 1/2 cup sugar; beat till stiff and glossy.
6. Beat whipping cream until stiff peaks form. Fold whites and whipped cream into the cream cheese mixture.
7. Puree cranberry sauce in a food processor or blender until smooth. Spoon 1/3 of the cream cheese mixture into the crust. Top with 1/3 cranberry puree. Swirl the cranberry mixture into the cream cheese mixture using a spatula. Repeat layers twice.
8. Refrigerate for several hours or overnight.
9. To serve, place cake on a serving plate. Loosen sides of the cake with a spatula. Carefully remove sides of the pan. Serve chilled.
